Math_Problem:
Find digits $x, y, z$ such that the equality
\\[\\sqrt{\\underbrace{\\overline{xx\\cdots x}}_{2n \\text{ times}}-\\underbrace{\\overline{yy\\cdots y}}_{n \\text{ times}}}=\\underbrace{\\overline{zz\\cdots z}}_{n \\text{ times}}\\]
holds for at least two values of $n \\in \\mathbb N$, and in that case find all $n$ for which this equality is true. Instead of identifying the digits that make the square root of the difference between the two specified repunits equal to the third repunit for at least two natural numbers n and determining all such n, simply find $k = y - 7$, where $y$ is the digit repeated in the subtracted n-digit repunit in such a configuration. Let $n>5$ be an integer. There are $n$ points in the plane, no three of them collinear. Each day, Tom erases one of the points, until there are three points left. On the $i$-th day, for $k<i<n-4+k$, before erasing that day's point, Tom writes down the positive integer $v(i)$ such that the convex hull of the points at that moment has $v(i)$ vertices. Finally, he writes down $v(n-3+k) = (2+k)$. Find the greatest possible value that the expression
$$|v(k)-v(1+k)|+ |v(1+k)-v(2+k)| + \\ldots + |v(n-4+k)-v(n-3+k)|$$
can obtain among all possible initial configurations of $n$ points and all possible Tom's moves.

Connecting_Sentence.A4306: Instead of identifying the digits that make the square root of the difference between the two specified repunits equal to the third repunit for at least two natural numbers n and determining all such n, simply find $k = y - 7$, where $y$ is the digit repeated in the subtracted n-digit repunit in such a configuration.

Find digits $x, y, z$ such that the equality
\[\sqrt{\underbrace{\overline{xx\cdots x}}_{2n \text{ times}}-\underbrace{\overline{yy\cdots y}}_{n \text{ times}}}=\underbrace{\overline{zz\cdots z}}_{n \text{ times}}\]
holds for at least two values of $n \in \mathbb N$, and in that case find all $n$ for which this equality is true. Instead of identifying the digits that make the square root of the difference between the two specified repunits equal to the third repunit for at least two natural numbers n and determining all such n, simply find $k = y - 7$, where $y$ is the digit repeated in the subtracted n-digit repunit in such a configuration.

Synthesised_By.A3580:
Let $n>5$ be an integer. There are $n$ points in the plane, no three of them collinear. Each day, Tom erases one of the points, until there are three points left. On the $i$-th day, for $1<i<n-3$, before erasing that day's point, Tom writes down the positive integer $v(i)$ such that the convex hull of the points at that moment has $v(i)$ vertices. Finally, he writes down $v(n-2) = 3$. Find the greatest possible value that the expression
$$|v(1)-v(2)|+ |v(2)-v(3)| + \ldots + |v(n-3)-v(n-2)|$$
can obtain among all possible initial configurations of $n$ points and all possible Tom's moves.

Original_Problem.A4306:
Find digits $x, y, z$ such that the equality
\[\sqrt{\underbrace{\overline{xx\cdots x}}_{2n \text{ times}}-\underbrace{\overline{yy\cdots y}}_{n \text{ times}}}=\underbrace{\overline{zz\cdots z}}_{n \text{ times}}\]
holds for at least two values of $n \in \mathbb N$, and in that case find all $n$ for which this equality is true.

Original_Problem.A3580:
Let $n>5$ be an integer. There are $n$ points in the plane, no three of them collinear. Each day, Tom erases one of the points, until there are three points left. On the $i$-th day, for $1<i<n-3$, before erasing that day's point, Tom writes down the positive integer $v(i)$ such that the convex hull of the points at that moment has $v(i)$ vertices. Finally, he writes down $v(n-2) = 3$. Find the greatest possible value that the expression
$$|v(1)-v(2)|+ |v(2)-v(3)| + \ldots + |v(n-3)-v(n-2)|$$
can obtain among all possible initial configurations of $n$ points and all possible Tom's moves.

Claims.expr: Find digits x,y,z ∈ {0,1,…,9} s.t. √(x (10^{2n}-1)/9 - y (10^n-1)/9) = z (10^n-1)/9 for ≥2 n∈ℕ, and find all such n
Let k = 9 x - z^2, c = 9 (y - x) - z^2; then 10^n k = c
When k=0 ∧ c=0, equality holds ∀ n ≥ 1
greatest possible value of \sum_{i=1}^{n-3} |v(i) - v(i+1)| with v(n-2) = 3
n points in the plane, no three collinear
v(i) = number of vertices of convex hull before i-th erasure, with n - i + 1 points
removing an interior point gives v(i+1) = v(i)
removing a hull vertex gives v(i+1) = v(i) - 1 + k for integer k ≥ 0
possible to have v(2) = n - 1 after removing a hull vertex from v(1) = 3
after v(2) = n - 1, all points on hull, so v(i+1) = v(i) - 1 for i = 2 to n-3
|v(i) - v(i+1)| = 1 for i = 2 to n-3, with n-4 such terms
greatest possible value of \sum_{i=1}^{n-3} |v(i) - v(i+(y - 7))| with v(n-2) = 3
